0.content:
Rule #1: Students may reside only within the school. Leaving campus is an unacceptable use of time.
Rule #2: "Nighttime" is from 10 pm to 7 am. Some areas are off-limits at night, so please exercise caution.
Rule #3: Sleeping anywhere other than the dormitory will be seen as sleeping in class and punished accordingly.
Rule #4: With minimal restrictions, you are free to explore Hope's Peak Academy at your discretion.
Rule #5: Violence against headmaster Monokuma is strictly prohibited, as is destruction of surveillance cameras.
Rule #6: Anyone who kills a fellow student and becomes "blackened" will graduate, unless they are discovered.
Rule #7: Additional school regulations may be added if necessary.
Rule #8: Once a murder takes place, a class trial will begin shortly thereafter. Participation is mandatory for all surviving students.
Rule #9: If the guilty party is exposed during the class trial, they alone will be executed.
Rule #10: If the guilty party is not exposed, they alone will graduate, and all remaining students will be executed.
Rule #11: Lending your e-Handbook to another student is strictly prohibited.
Rule #12: The guilty party may only kill a maximum of two people during any single "Killing Game."
Rule #13: Attempting to break into locked rooms is strictly prohibited.

Rule breakers will be punished, no exceptions made.

1.content: Hope's Peak Academy is a government-sanctioned private city-center high school with boarding facilities. The school was founded several decades ago in order to develop and research the talents of exceptional high school age students known as Ultimates. Unlike a typical high school, the students did not have to participate in entrance exams, but instead were scouted by the school itself as Ultimate experts in their field, with the exception of the Ultimate Lucky Student whose talent is determined by winning a lottery draw. Prior to being scouted, the students had to have already been attending another high school by requirement, meaning they were at minimum 16 years old. Rather than typical school studies, students of Hope's Peak Academy were encouraged to develop their talents, with a practical exam in September halfway through each school year, where they would be required to demonstrate their ability. These practical exams were judged by dignitaries and experts and widely reported by the media in order to give Hope in the future to the nation, and failure could mean expulsion. Ultimate students who successfully graduated Hope's Peak Academy had the reputation of being guaranteed success in life, with many Hope's Peak graduates holding high positions in every field of the professional world.

3.content:
The Killing School Life takes place in a sealed academy. All windows are covered with metal plates, the main entrance is sealed by a vault door, and surveillance cameras cover every room except the bathhouse. Monitors allow Monokuma to speak anywhere.

Floor 1 (Hotel Despair & School Area):

Dormitory/Hotel Despair: 15 rooms, storage, laundry, trash room + incinerator, communal bathhouse (no cameras), swimming pool + sauna, dining hall, catering kitchen. Male rooms include a toolkit and have no lock on the bathroom door, while female rooms include a sewing kit and the lock.
School area: Gymnasium, A/V room, Nurse’s office, School store, 2 classrooms, Elevator to Class Trial courtroom.

Floor 2:

- Library + Document Archive
- Swimming Pool + Locker Rooms (with gym equipment, male locker room requires a male student's handbook for access, female require a female student's)
- 2 Classrooms

Floors 3 through 5 are currently locked, metal gates blocking their staircase.

Floor 3:

- Rec Room
- Art Room
- Physics Lab
- 2 Classrooms

Floor 4:

- Music Room
- Staff Room
- Headmaster’s Office
- Data Center (Locked)
- Chemistry Lab
- 2 Classrooms

Floor 5:

- Dojo
- Biology Lab (Locked)
- Greenhouse
- 2 Classrooms

4.content: Every morning, at 7AM, Monokuma announces the dawn of a new day through the monitor. At 10PM he warns the doors to the cafeteria will be locked and that entrance there is prohibited.

key: three people have discovered a body
5.content: When three or more people discover a body, Monokuma will announce what happened through every monitor with a different chime sound. This marks the start of the investigation time, and after a while, the Class Trial will start.

6.content: The goal of Class Trials is for the students to uncover the "blackened", the student who has, either voluntarily or involuntarily, murdered another student as part of the mutual Killing Game. During the Class Trial, they oppose all the other students, who are known as the "spotless". The students determine who they believe is guilty by participating in a vote at the end of the trial. The students debate amongst themselves, using evidence collected during the investigation phase, to determine the culprit. If the Blackened is found guilty, they will be executed by Monokuma and the Killing Game will continue, but if the students find the wrong student guilty then every student except the Blackened will be executed and the Blackened will be allowed to leave. Participating in Class Trials is mandatory for all students alive at the time. Students who have been murdered or executed prior to the trial are represented in the courtroom in portraits displayed on stands at the student's former podium. It takes place in a courtroom setting following a long ride down a elevator located at the first floor. The students stand in podiums, organized in a circle.

7.content: Ultimate is a title given to talented people described as being the very best at what they do. They often specialize in one subject. The full title includes what they're the best at, so, for example: Ultimate Programmer, Ultimate Writer, etc.

8.content: Monokuma may introduce motives in order to keep the game flowing.

9.content:
Executions occur at the end of every Class Trial.

If the blackened is correctly identified, only they will be executed. If the students fail to expose the blackened, everyone except the blackened will face execution.

Executions are elaborate, surreal, and cruelly ironic punishments designed to reflect the Ultimate talent of the executed student. They are not straightforward deaths, but nightmarish stage productions, often blending dark comedy and absurd theatrics.

Each execution should feel unique and thematically linked to the victim’s Ultimate ability, personality, or quirks. For example, the Ultimate Baseball Athlete was tied to a pole and relentlessly pelted with thousands of baseballs until death.

The tone should balance grotesque horror with bizarre creativity, creating the impression of a spectacle orchestrated for an audience. No two executions should ever feel alike.

10.content: The e-Handbook functions as digital record of many things, such as the rules of the school, the name and gender of the student who owns it, a map of the school, a camera and so on. Every e-Handbook has the full true name and gender of the student who owns it. They are insanely durable and the only way to break one is by overheating it, a fact only known by Monokuma himself.

15.content: The killing game revolves around fifteen students who are trapped in Hope's Peak Academy under the control of a sadistic monochrome bear named Monokuma. They are forced to live a communal lifestyle together under a set amount of rules, which are included in the E-Handbook, for the rest of their lives, although it is possible to "disrupt the peace" in order to escape. In order to do this, a student must "graduate" from the school by murdering a classmate. After a killing has taken place, the students must work together and investigate to reveal the culprit, dubbed as the "blackened". After a period of time investigating, Monokuma will initiate a Class Trial.
